behaviors.statement: POST /v1/orders/{id}/discounts with a discount value in the range [1, 90] returns a 2xx response and the response body reflects a recalculated order total
POST /v1/orders/{id}/discounts with discount value exactly 1 (lower boundary) is accepted
POST /v1/orders/{id}/discounts with discount value exactly 90 (upper boundary) is accepted
POST /v1/orders/{id}/discounts with discount value 0 (one below lower bound) returns HTTP 400
POST /v1/orders/{id}/discounts with discount value 91 (one above upper bound) returns HTTP 400
POST /v1/orders/{id}/discounts with a negative discount value returns HTTP 400

open_questions: AC-3 ('stacking TBD'): no expected outcome defined for discount stacking — behavior is unresolvable; tests cannot be generated until specified
AC-1: 'total recalculated' does not name the response field or document whether recalculation is synchronous — cannot assert a specific field without clarification
AC-2: the HTTP 400 response body schema is not specified — error message field name and format are unknown
AC-2: does 'out-of-range' include non-numeric inputs (null, string, float)? No behavior is stated for those cases

tests.name: PROJ-301: extend PROJ-88 — assert recalculated order total in 2xx response
PROJ-301: lower boundary discount value 1 is accepted (2xx)
PROJ-301: upper boundary discount value 90 is accepted (2xx)
PROJ-301: discount value 0 (one below lower bound) returns 400
PROJ-301: discount value 91 (one above upper bound) returns 400

open_questions: AC-3 ('stacking TBD'): no expected outcome defined for discount stacking — behavior is unresolvable; tests cannot be generated until specified

AC-1: 'total recalculated' does not name the response field or document whether recalculation is synchronous — cannot assert a specific field without clarification; 'extend PROJ-88' scenario is a test.todo skeleton pending this resolution
AC-2: the HTTP 400 response body schema is not specified — error message field name and format are unknown; S3, S4, S5 assert HTTP status code only
AC-2: does 'out-of-range' include non-numeric inputs (null, string, float)? No behavior is stated for those cases — no scenarios generated

contract.strategy: One canonical order fixture (order-proj301-001, total_cents=20000) provides a known pre-discount baseline shared across all six scenarios. Discount cases are enumerated per scenario with the pct field matching the existing orders-api contract convention; boundary values 1 and 90 cover S1/S2, out-of-range values 0/91/-1 cover S3/S4/S5, and the extend-PROJ-88 case carries fixme=true pending OQ-2 resolution. assert_body=false is set on all 400 cases pending OQ-3. All data is synthetic with clearly-fake domains; no PII.

scenarios.title: PROJ-301: extend PROJ-88 — assert recalculated order total in 2xx response
scenarios.data_needs: synthetic order with known pre-discount total; discount in [1,90]; response field name TBD — test.fixme skeleton pending OQ-2

PROJ-301: discount value 0 (one below lower bound) returns 400

scenarios.title: PROJ-301: discount value 91 (one above upper bound) returns 400
scenarios.data_needs: synthetic order id; discount=91; assert status only — 400 body schema unknown (OQ-3)

scenarios.data_needs: synthetic order id; discount=-1; assert status only — 400 body schema unknown (OQ-3)

contract.rationale: Contract change in openapi/orders.yaml affects downstream consumer (web-storefront-ui). Existing test coverage for orders endpoints exists; tests need updates to reflect new contract. Cross-repo impact confirmed.
